reviews.review_title: This should be taken off the store
reviews.review_description:
I sincerely hope this app gets removed from the store for breaching Meta Terms/Rules of Conduct. They scammed all the users who paid for it, by removing content that they PAID FOR from the game.
And now they come up with a lousy explanation that they want to support real music/artists. Sure, how about you support it by paying the licensing fees for the new content with the money that was stolen and already TAKEN OUT OF OUR POCKETS and actually give those new songs "for free" to the users who made you money.
What they did is illegal, unethical and they should be held accountable. 
Do NOT buy THIS!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing how you feel, and we're sorry this update upset you.

We know it's frustrating to see songs you enjoyed leave the game. This was not an easy choice for us. We retired the older tracks so we could move toward licensed music from real artists, and we understand that change was not what everyone wanted.

We also want to share that the same update added two new licensed albums, 10 tracks from real artists, given to all players for free. And we have kept adding more since then, with another 5 tracks in our latest update. That brings 15 new tracks to everyone at no extra cost, and there is still more on the way.

Thank you again for taking the time to tell us your thoughts. We hope you will give the new music a try.

reviews.review_description: Most of the songs are "okay", but the more enjoyable ones were taken off. There is a decent amount of lagging in the game, and sometimes its like the game only registers you hit the object if your punch is fully in your view. This makes it annoying as i have to turn another view direction to keep up with the game, and in doing so, will "miss" the punch dispite seeing my hand come into contact with the flying object. Seems counter intuitive. 2 stars for a good workout, and nothing else because this game is kinda mid .

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to share this.

About the music: the songs that were removed were AI-generated, and we decided to retire them to support real music and real artists. It wasn't an easy call, but we felt it was the right direction for the game. In their place we've added free licensed albums from real artists, with more tracks on the way, and we hope they click with you.

On the gameplay side, the lag and the hit registration you mentioned — where a punch only counts when your hand is fully in view — are exactly the kind of issues we want to fix. We'll look into both carefully.

Feedback like this really helps us make the game better.

reviews.review_title: They cut so many songs from the game.
reviews.review_description:
Brought the game and enjoyed it, it had plenty of songs with lots of variety of music. 
I came on today to find out they have cut pretty much every song I enjoyed, what is left is really bad, slow, downbeat music that hardly fits a rhythm game. They've pretty much scammed people who actually enjoy the game out of content to pander to the odd person that hears it has AI, leaves a anti-AI negative review, then never plays the game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing Vibe Punch!

We really understand how you feel, and this wasn't an easy decision for us either. Removing those tracks was part of our move to fully support real music and real artists going forward. We know that change is frustrating when it takes away songs you loved, and we're sorry for that.

The two new licensed albums added in 1.6.0 are just the start. We're actively working on bringing in more tracks, and we hear you on wanting more upbeat, high-energy music that really fits the rhythm combat feel. More is on the way.

Thanks again for sticking with us and sharing your honest thoughts. It really helps us make the game better.
Keep Punching!

reviews.review_description: Disappointed that the devs have removed the AI-generated music because the style of that music appealed to me and worked well with the game. Now the available track list is about 25% of what it was, and most of the music no longer appeals to me. Considering that I paid for the game, this is a terrible bait-and-switch. They got my money!

I absolutely love this game, beat saber was my favourite then synth rider but then I played this and it’s become my new favourite, I work up a sweat more on this one, the one down side for me is I’d like to be able to add known songs I like, from great 80s tunes to Eminem, just needs better music, I like this game enough not to deduct a star just because of this but please give us the choice of our own music options,

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing Vibe Punch! 

It really means a lot to hear that our game has become your new favorite. Knowing that you're working up a good sweat while playing makes us really happy. That's exactly the kind of experience we want to deliver.

We totally hear you on wanting more music choices. The wish to play your own favorite tracks is something we understand very well, and it's something we think about a lot. A custom map editor is on our roadmap, which would open up a lot of possibilities down the road. We don't have a specific date to share just yet, but it's a direction we're committed to.

In the meantime, we're also continuing to expand our track library with new albums, so there will be more music to enjoy as we grow. Thank you for your support and for not letting this hold back your rating. It really means a lot to the whole team. 

Keep Punching!

reviews.review_description: Why did y'all delete a lot of the songs? A lot of the songs I liked playing. I can understand if it was a rights issue but I don't think that's the case. I hardly play now because the selection in music isn't great since a lot was taken out. I really loved Without you. A lot of others too but I didn't get the titles on it. If y'all could, bring them back. Something we can actually punch and dance to.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for spending time with VibePunch and sharing your honest thoughts with us. We truly appreciate that you cared enough about the music selection to write this review.

We completely understand your disappointment about the songs that were removed.  it wasn’t an easy decision for us either. However, after much consideration, we truly believe this was the right direction to take for the long-term vision of VibePunch. We kindly ask for your understanding on this matter.

Right now, VibePunch includes 35 real music tracks across multiple albums, and our team is preparing to add even more in future updates to bring back the variety and fun you mentioned. 

Thank you again for sharing your passion for the game — it truly helps us make VibePunch better with every beat.

Keep Punching!

reviews.review_title: Too much AI generated music, low effort
reviews.review_description: First off, I really like the game. The first 2 volumes of music are all great and poppy. However, the next like 12 volumes are all AI generated music and attributed to some guy. They all sound bad and are not really fun. The other problem I've got with it is there's no actual difficulty. Every difficulty per song  has the same number of enemies and notes on and the slider only changes how much HP you get per level. Will happily change my rating if they ever put more effort into it.

developer_response.body:
Updated (2026-03-11) – Version 1.6.0

• Two licensed albums have been added, introducing 10 new tracks from real artists.
• The game now features 35 real music tracks.
• All previous AI-generated tracks have been retired as part of our transition to real licensed music.
• More real tracks from real artists are coming in future updates.


Just to clarify, Albums 1–4 are all officially licensed real music, not AI-generated.
The game currently offers 55 tracks total (25 licensed songs + 30 bonus tracks), and 5 more real licensed tracks for Boss Mode are coming in the next update, all at no extra cost.

We’re fully focused on expanding the real licensed music library, and all new songs added in future updates will be licensed tracks as well.

In the current version, the 6 bonus albums (30 tracks) were included to provide more content variety, and we understand your concern — we’ll continue improving both music quality and gameplay feel.

We’re also actively working on refining difficulty balance and enemy patterns, so each level feels more dynamic and challenging.

Thank you again for playing Vibe Punch. We’ll keep making it better — one update, one punch at a time. 👊

reviews.review_description: This game is a lot of fun and a fantastic workout. I use it almost every day now as a cardio workout. After the update they got rid of the AI tracks which is great but I hope they add back some other albums soon as there’s only 6 now. There’s a few great tracks. But a lot of them are too slow and don’t match the vibe of the game. How about some upbeat edm and more metal etc. take a page from the likes of Pistol Whip and Beat Saber etc. they have tons of great songs. So overall a lot of fun and highly recommend but please keep some updates and improvements coming !

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing Vibe Punch! 

We're really glad to hear that Vibe Punch has become part of your daily workout routine! That makes us super happy. Having fun while getting a great workout is exactly what we were going for.

We know 6 albums are not a lot right now, but we are planning to add a lot more tracks in the future, so please look forward to it.

Your feedback really helps us know what the community wants, so thank you for sharing it with us.

Keep punching!

reviews.review_title: Great game but AI-generated music is bad
reviews.review_description: Actually it’s a game I play on a daily basis because it’s a good physical exercise but the AI-generated music is so tasteless and low effort as someone else reviewed in another comment. I wish they could just remove those songs and focus on adding more music created by humans. My favorite collection is the volume 4, I’ll keep playing the game but I hope they’ll improve on that matter.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the thoughtful feedback, and we’re really glad to hear the game has become part of your daily exercise routine!

Regarding the AI-generated tracks you mentioned, we completely understand the concerns. In our recent update (v1.6.0 – March 11, 2026), all AI-generated tracks have been removed as part of our commitment to focusing on real music. We also added two newly licensed albums featuring 10 tracks from real artists, bringing the total number of real music tracks in the game to 35.

We truly appreciate players like you who took the time to share honest feedback, and we’re continuing to work on expanding the library with more music created by real artists in future updates.

Keep Punching!

reviews.review_description: I got this with horizon + trying games that seemed fun, but this game is what i needed its like the fitness apps, beatsaber, and pistol whip had a beautiful baby, the songs i found were fun i like the songs in the album with the song "Pillow" with game you can tell is trying thier best to reach greatness, The simplisity of the dufficulties being a change of health gives it that push to improve your skills and it was just fun to play, i do wish there were collab songs from artists like AJR or Jack Stauber as they would fit into this enviroment and play style the game has but things like that are up to the devs so keep going the way your going 10/10 game so far for what we get normally.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for enjoying VibePunch!
Reading your words truly means a lot to our team. We’re really glad to hear that you felt the rhythm and energy we worked hard to bring to the game. Our goal has always been to make something simple, fun, and rewarding to play, so your feedback really makes our efforts worthwhile.

It’s great to know that you liked the album that includes “Pillow”! We currently have 35 real music tracks in the game, and we’re continuing to expand that list over time so players can experience even more variety and moods while punching to the beat.

Thank you again for taking the time to leave such a kind review. We’ll keep working hard to make every punch, dodge, and rhythm feel even better.

Keep Punching!

reviews.review_title: My shoulders and arms are burning!
reviews.review_description:
I bought this game on sale as I wasn't sure if I would like it or not. I wanted something fun but would also give me a good workout to help me get fitter. This I think this very much fits the bill. At times it's hard to keep up and my shoulders especially are aching from all the punching. :)

I've seen a lot said about AI music however that music has now been replaced in the game.  I can't comment on the older music as it's before my time with the game but every track I've tried so far sounds great and works really well with the music.

Graphics and sound are solid too, really well done on this.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review, we really appreciate it.

We’re glad to hear you’re enjoying the game and finding it both fun and engaging. Creating something that people can come back to and enjoy consistently is something we care a lot about, so it’s great to see that coming through.

We’ve also been making changes and improvements across different parts of the game, including the music and overall experience, and we’ll continue to refine things step by step based on feedback.

We have more updates planned going forward, so we hope you’ll keep an eye on how the game continues to evolve. Thanks again for playing and for taking the time to share your thoughts.

Keep Punching!

reviews.review_description:
This game is great! Get what you want out of it. Just for fun, play like normal. I was able to get good cardio out of it moving my whole body! Great alternative way to burn some calories, or just having fun.

Music so far is great. While I personally would feel indifferent with a mix of AI and human music (I would actually prefer more variety), I can certainly respect that the developers listen to community feedback and make change. Considering most VR games are from small developers, this change is probably much more demanding than most people give credit for.

Respect to the devs! This game is amazing!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words, we really appreciate it.

We’re happy to hear that you’re enjoying the game both as a fun experience and as a way to get some good cardio in. That balance is something we care a lot about.

About the music, we totally understand your point. We originally added some AI-generated tracks to help expand the music library, since licensing more songs can be challenging for a small team. But over time, we received quite a bit of feedback from players who didn’t enjoy those tracks. We also felt it was important to focus more on music made by real artists, as it better fits the direction we want for the game.

So we decided to remove the AI tracks and move forward with that in mind. We’re continuing to work on adding more variety, and your feedback really helps guide us.

Thanks again for your support—it means a lot to us.

Keep Punching!

reviews.review_description:
I’ve been playing this for a few months and it’s one of the few things that has actually made exercising fun (not normally my thing). For context, I’m a 66-year-old woman who only got a VR headset last summer. I started on easy and have gradually moved most songs up to medium, with a few perfect scores.

First, I know some people are bothered by the AI music, but I’m not. Honestly, unless it’s from the “late 1900s,” I probably wouldn’t know whether it’s real or AI anyway. I mostly just avoid the heavier synth tracks. If you do plan to add licensed music and sell packs, you might consider some of your older players and include some 60s–90s music. Otherwise I’ll probably keep using whatever songs are free.

As for difficulty and exercise, my Apple Watch counts this as cardio. I even play it while standing on a rebounder trampoline (not a recommendation) which works some lower muscles and helps with balance.

What I’m saying is: this is a really good workout for those of us who aren’t trying to compete, chase stats, or worry about who wrote the song. We just want a fun way to move.

So by all means make changes, add or remove songs, and keep improving things—but don’t get so focused on making it “better” that you accidentally take away what already works.

Otherwise, thanks for a fun game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your thoughtful and encouraging review!

We’re truly happy to hear that Vibe Punch has made exercising enjoyable for you—that means a lot to us. It’s especially great to hear how you’ve progressed from easy to medium and even achieved perfect scores. That’s genuinely impressive.

We also really appreciate your perspective on the music. As someone who was born in the 1980s, I personally share a sense of nostalgia for older styles of music as well. As we continue to expand our music library, we’ll aim to include a wider variety of styles and eras so that players with different tastes can all find something they enjoy.

As you mentioned, Vibe Punch has a variety of updates planned ahead, including adding more music, and will continue to evolve over time. At the same time, one of the things we are most careful about is exactly what you pointed out—making sure that in the process of adding new features, we don’t take away from the core fun of the game. We’ll keep that firmly in mind as we continue development, so please stay tuned and continue to keep an eye on us.

Thank you again for playing Vibe Punch and for sharing your experience with us!

Keep Punching!

reviews.review_description: ADDITIONAL REVIEW 2/14/2026- I am enjoying other music. So I am changing my previous rating for the music from a 5 to a 7. I need more tips on how to improve my score. This app gives a decent cardio workout. I’m not quite sure on the settings and what a few of them actually mean. It would be great if there was a practice option where I could adjust the settings just to see what works best for me. I don’t mind if music is AI-generated, however. I would much prefer the option of using music that I’ve uploaded to my headset…game-changer. Otherwise, the music gets a 5/10. Perhaps categorizing music by genres would be helpful. I mainly use Audioshield which allows me to choose from my music but, it doesn’t provide the intensity that I need. I definitely look forward to seeing this app add other features. Overall, I give it 8/10. I’m definitely getting hooked! Thanks. I just read that it’s best to not wear earbuds…noted. I do very well with timing but, was concerned. I’ll try it without the AirPods. My favorite song is RNA.

Updated (2026-03-11) – Version 1.6.0

reviews.review_title: Sad they took the fun song off
reviews.review_description: The game is fun. But I’m super sad now because they took the run, run, run song off. I liked playing that song. Wish they hadn’t removed that one. Now they have a whole bunch of songs by artists I’ve never even heard of put songs on there that everyone knows……

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback, and we’re really glad to hear you’re enjoying the game.

In version 1.6.0 we removed all AI-generated tracks and began transitioning the game to licensed music from real artists. Thank you for playing Vibe Punch and for sharing your thoughts with us.

reviews.review_title: Good game but music is meh
reviews.review_description:
I enjoy the gameplay but the music isnt my thing. Was expecting the dancy Monstercat of Beatsaber or 3force synthwave of Synth riders kinda vibe, but apart from the 1 token rock album, it's all pretty much funky hip hop or pop, which Im not a fan of. I can understand why people are unhappy with the AI music being removed if this is what is left. 

Didnt refund the game as it does have a lot of potential and boss mode is great, if the music improves and expands beyond hip hop/AI is put back/we get custom maps Ill give the game another star.

developer_response.body:

Music is a big part of what makes the game fun, and we know how much it shapes the experience. We're working hard to add more tracks and bring more variety to the library, so there's something that fits everyone's taste. We've got more on the way, and we're always looking at how to make each update better than the last.

Glad you're enjoying boss mode. We put a lot of work into it, and there's plenty more we want to do to keep the game growing and improving over time. Stick around to see what's coming.

reviews.review_title: A bit of a long one, but bear with me
reviews.review_description:
I understand that people are upset about the inclusion of AI-generated music, but can we at least appreciate that they openly disclose it in the UI? It's not like they're trying to sneak it in under the radar. There's a decent amount of real music to play for how new the game is anyway.

One of the highly rated reviews mentions difficulty not changing anything other than HP, so I tried playing on a lower difficulty. It seems like the notes spawn earlier to give you more time to react because I found it noticeably easier to keep up. I will concede that it looked like the same number of bullets and such, which I do think should change.

With that out of the way, as someone who's been playing rhythm games for about a decade, Vibe Punch is pretty well put together. The notes are mapped in a way that feels good to play and hit combos on. I like putting my own spin on it too since you can use your hands interchangeably (though it could be interesting to see a mode/modifier that makes each bullet assigned to a specific hand for added difficulty). Bear in mind that it is a bit physically demanding as far as VR games go - I burn through my stamina quicker on this than I do on something like Beat Saber. For me that's part of the charm, but it might not be for everyone.


P.S. + questions for the devs (if you read this):
- The description says "dodge deadly bobms" instead of "bombs"
- Is scoring based on timing *and* force, or is it just force and managing to hit notes? It feels like I'm getting perfect notes when I'm a little off on my timing
- Is there any plan to add hand tracking? It would translate pretty well, the only controls you need are navigating the UI and moving your hands. It would definitely feel more satisfying too.

developer_response.body:
Updated (2026-03-11) – Version 1.6.0

• Two licensed albums have been added, introducing 10 new tracks from real artists.
• The game now features 35 real music tracks.
• All previous AI-generated tracks have been retired as part of our transition to real licensed music.
• More real tracks from real artists are coming in future updates.



Hello! Thank you so much for enjoying Vibe Punch.

We truly appreciate the time and care you put into writing such a thoughtful review. Feedback like yours really motivates our team to keep improving and developing the game further.

Vibe Punch currently features 25 real tracks created by actual artists.
As a small indie studio, it can be quite challenging for us to license a wide variety of great songs from real musicians due to financial limitations. Because of this, we were worried that players might eventually feel the 25 licensed tracks were not enough to enjoy over a long period of time. So, we decided to add some AI-generated tracks that we could include without licensing costs.

We hope you can think of the AI tracks as bonus content and simply enjoy them as an extra.
All AI-generated tracks are clearly labeled as such in-game, so please keep that in mind when playing them.

Regarding the difficulty levels, we’re aware of the concern that the three difficulty modes mainly adjust timing and speed, while the number of bullets and bombs does not change. Our team fully understands this point.
We will discuss internally whether it’s possible to improve higher difficulties so that more bullets and additional elements are introduced.

Regarding your questions:
	•	Thank you for letting us know about the typo in “Bombs.” We will correct it right away.
	•	Combo and Perfect judgments are purely timing-based.
        •	If you hit a bullet with enough force, your hit will become a Power Shot. A Power Shot gives you more points than a normal hit.
	•	Hand-tracking is not currently in development, but we will review the possibility of adding it in the future.

Vibe Punch is constantly evolving, and our team is committed to improving the weaker parts of the game and adding new features to make it even richer and more enjoyable. We promise to share your feedback with the team and take it into consideration as we continue development.

Once again, thank you so much for your meaningful review and for enjoying the game.

Keep Punching!

I missed so much！

reviews.review_description:
Literally half of the entire song library is AI generated, even going so far as to list the people who generated the garbage as artists. I'll change it to 2 stars at least if you warn people at the top of the description so they can see before they buy how much is AI.

Edit: It is better to not respond to review at all than it is to AI generate a response.

developer_response.body:
Updated (2026-03-11) – Version 1.6.0

• Two licensed albums have been added, introducing 10 new tracks from real artists.
• The game now features 35 real music tracks.
• All previous AI-generated tracks have been retired as part of our transition to real licensed music.
• More real tracks from real artists are coming in future updates.


Thank you for taking the time to share your honest feedback — we truly understand your concerns.

Vibe Punch was created by a small indie studio, and securing high-quality licensed music that can be used commercially is genuinely challenging for teams like ours, especially during early development. Because of this, AI-generated tracks were included as part of the game’s original structure from the beginning — they were not added later.

We have considered removing AI tracks entirely. However, we also have players who genuinely enjoy them, so we are working to find a balanced approach that respects both preferences.

Currently, the game includes 55 total tracks, with 25 licensed songs from real artists. We are also preparing to add 15 more licensed tracks in the near future, which will significantly expand the real-music portion of the library.

Based on community feedback, we are updating the game structure:
Licensed music albums will become the main progression path, while AI-generated tracks will be moved into optional bonus albums that unlock after completing the main content.

Our goal is for AI tracks to feel like additional bonus content rather than the core experience.

We genuinely appreciate feedback like yours — it helps us improve and shape the future direction of Vibe Punch.

reviews.review_title: This is the best new rhythm game 2025
reviews.review_description:
I use VR for fitness primarily and this is one of the best looking one. If you’ve been playing Beat Saber, pistol whipped this is something with comparable visuals and pretty nice beats.  (At least from the first few albums) 

The gameplay is a little difficult and can seem repetitive, but I still think it’s a good game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing VibePunch and for taking the time to share your review! We’re really glad to hear that you’ve been enjoying the game, especially as part of your VR fitness routine. It’s wonderful to know that the visuals and beats have made a strong impression — the team worked hard on every detail so it’s very rewarding to hear that it comes through when you play.

We understand that the gameplay can feel a bit challenging and, at times, repetitive. Feedback like yours is truly valuable . We’ll be discussing this with the team to see how we can continue improving balance and variety in future updates.

Thank you again for your support and for sharing your honest thoughts. We hope you keep finding new tracks and moments that make every session more fun and energetic.

Keep Punching!

reviews.review_title: Great base, but needs additional work.
reviews.review_description:
Great looks and basic gameplay. The first volume of music is great, after that (especially volume 3 and on) the music is hit or miss, and mostly unremarkable. 

What really stands out in the later volumes is the lack of synchronization between the music beats and the punching dynamic. It is weirdly off, in both the physical movement and the use of really crappy sound effects. This is disappointing because the first volume nails pretty much everything, but then it seems they just shoveled out AI generated tracks and play patterns with no effort.

If they can duplicate the effort and polish that exists in the first volume of music in later volumes this will be a game worth purchasing, but if they keep just dumping in low effort volumes to pad out the offerings it's only worth to pick up on heavy discount.

I’m not usually one for ratings or reviews, but I wanted to share my thoughts on this game. It’s incredibly fun and amazing—it really gets you going and lifts your spirits. It’s a great game to enjoy and exercise with. One thing I’d love is if you could add more glove skins. Overall, it’s an amazing game and so much fun!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing VibePunch and for taking the time to leave your thoughts! We're truly happy to hear that you're enjoying the game and that it's bringing you both fun and energy—that's exactly the experience we hoped to create. reviews.review_description:
Vibe Punch has a surprisingly deep scoring system.
You earn bonus points by punching back the incoming shots and destroying the pillars that appear — and their positions change every time.
To get a high score, you really need to return every shot and aim for a perfect run. But if you focus too much on the pillars, you’ll make mistakes quickly.

Returned shots curve through the air, so you have to read their trajectory and punch with enough power to keep the flow going.
It’s simple at first, but the more you play, the more depth you discover. A very satisfying rhythm game!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for playing VibePunch and leaving such a thoughtful review! We’re truly delighted to hear that you’ve found depth and enjoyment in our scoring system. It means a lot to us that the challenge of mastering shot returns and aiming for those perfect runs is coming through in your gameplay experience.

It’s great to know that you appreciate how the pillar patterns and shot trajectories make each playthrough unique. We wanted those elements to encourage skill development and reward careful observation.

Your feedback helps motivate us to keep improving and finding ways to make the game even more engaging for everyone. If you have any ideas or things you’d like to see in future updates, feel free to let us know! We always look forward to hearing from dedicated players like you.

Thanks again for investing your time and sharing your impressions. Keep Punching!

reviews.review_description:
After weeks of non-stop ads on social media about this game, I gave  it a try. I have to say it's a disappointment when you start the game. The boss fights is the only great thing about this game. It needs more of it. The regular volume music it just feels random. The more you go into the ai generated music, the more out of place it feels. I don't mind the ai music being used. The selection that was chosen feels like they picked whatever it gave them and put it in the game to give it content. I preferred good quality AI music than 30 more AI music at random like the coffee  ai music I was like What?! It feels like they ask chatgpt, "Make me a music about drinking coffee. lmao 

This game feels like it was made to tell a story with the boss fight on each volume. That's why this feels much different and better than other similar games. Every time I replay the boss fights, it just feels like a story driven concert. For example, the first boss is a style of rave style music. All of the first volume should have been a similar style of music leading up to the boss. You are fighting a DJ robot for crying out loud it's the perfect opportunity to have all the 1st volume be dj mix that leads to him. Volume 2 was mostly rock driven music it should end with a boss with the same style, heavy rock boss music. Make the dj robot change to a punk style robot, give it that feeling that everything flows together. 
Incredibox had this idea that it would unlock a small story after a combination of beats. Making it feel like a story that we created.
Fortnite had those music video events that combine artists' albums into mixed music videos.

This game has a very unique opportunity to create an amazing game. Not every AI song needs vocals. This game just needs more rhythm in its AI music. Since the main character is a dj robot, take inspiration from how real djs create and mix different music to create unique styles.

reviews.review_description:
I’ve been really enjoying Vibe Punch because of its Freeform, gameplay and catchy custom songs. I was initially afraid that it was gonna be the same old, same old like all the other rhythm games but it feels different enough. 

Here are some ideas for future updates:
- at the end of a song, it would be cool if it tallied up your different types of punches, like right hooks, upper cuts, jabs, etc. then you could build on this and incorporate a mode where you were required to do a certain amount of punch types or combos 
- I’d also like to see a mode where you can use the power of your punch as a way to get more points or to fill up a meter of sorts. Such as a meter decreasing as the song goes on and the only way to fill, it is by punching, but how harder you punch the more it fills
- I like the simplicity as it is now, but another mode that would add more enemy types and encourage you to do more types of maneuvers, like double punch and fist slams. 
- of course, we would welcome more songs. I like the song style for volume 10 so more of those please.

Keep up the good work and I look forward to future updates.

Hey, thanks for your honest feedback — it really helps us improve the game.

We’re glad you enjoy the gameplay!  
Currently Vibe Punch includes **20 fully licensed real tracks**  and 5 more licensed songs are coming soon.

We also added **30 bonus tracks** that we created with a lot of time and care.

reviews.review_description:
Thank you for the trial!

I was on the fence because I already own so many rhythm games, but wow, this is by far the best I’ve ever played.

The graphics are incredible.
The music is phenomenal.

reviews.review_description:
VibePunch hits different.
Every punch lands on beat and fires back at enemies — it’s rhythm + combat in one slick package.
The music? Stylish, vibey, and totally different from the usual rhythm game tracks — it’s got that cool, underground energy that just hits right.
Visually, it’s next-level — bold, cinematic, and super immersive.

And the fists? Let’s just say I’ve never felt this cool throwing punches in VR.
When enemies come charging at you and you smash them right on the drop? Seriously satisfying.

Honestly, I’m enjoying this even more than Beat Saber.
If you’re into rhythm games with edge, this one’s a must-play. 🎶💥

I love dancing, my bf’s all about games, and this is our new fave thing to do together. Total couple core 💃🎮

The songs are SUCH a vibe. Each one has a diff map so it keeps things fresh.

reviews.review_title: Didn’t expect to love this so much 😂
reviews.review_description:
This game is honestly such a blast. You’re just punching to the beat, but with the cool visuals and music, it feels more like dancing and fighting at the same time — in the best way.

I started playing just to check it out and ended up sweaty and smiling like an idiot. 😂 It’s such a fun way to move your body without feeling like “ugh I’m working out.”

reviews.review_description:
WoW~ There have been many changes in 1.0 version!
Fitness games can be something you want to avoid on tiring days, but Vibe Punch is more of a rhythm game-based fitness game, so it's not a burden!
Unlike Beat Saber, you're not tied to fixed rules, and you can freely and refreshingly throw punches in any direction you want, such as hooks, uppercuts, and straights, without the boundaries of left and right hands!
So even if you play the same song again, you can enjoy it with a different play style.
It would be great if various languages and custom modes were supported :)

reviews.review_description:
It has the potential but, for me, it’s too hard to see the notes on the rails when there are more than 3 on one plane. 

It’s very similar to synth riders on force mode. Just not as easy to distinguish notes. Very frustrating to miss the last one on long rails, especially as there is only 3 lives so a silly mistake can end the game. The no fail means that you can bypass this but (to me) there is no sense of achievement passing a level this way. 

Also I don’t know if it was meant to happen but if I die when the background is quickly flashing then it actually carries it into the main menu so it makes it very hard to play as it gets a bit much on the eyes with the constant flickering of colour

Overall it’s a good premise and I look forward to seeing where it can go 

TLDR: gameplay is good, needs work on the visuals. Would benefit from licensing music

developer_response.body:
Updated (2026-03-11) – Version 1.6.0

• Two licensed albums have been added, introducing 10 new tracks from real artists.
• The game now features 35 real music tracks.
• All previous AI-generated tracks have been retired as part of our transition to real licensed music.
• More real tracks from real artists are coming in future updates.


Thank you so much for sharing your honest and thoughtful feedback about VibePunch. We really appreciate the time and detail you put into your review—hearing directly from players is how we make the game better.

We understand how it can be difficult to see the notes when several are on the same rail, especially compared to other rhythm games. Clear visual cues are important for an enjoyable experience, so we’re discussing your comments with our team to explore possible changes that could help make the notes easier to distinguish, especially on long sections where missing one can feel particularly punishing.

If you find that having only three lives is too challenging, you can increase the number of lives by lowering the difficulty setting in the options. This way, the game can feel more forgiving and give you more room to practice and enjoy the tracks without feeling frustrated by small mistakes.

And, Thank you as well for raising the issue with the flashing colors carrying over into the main menu. We’re sorry this affected your experience and want you to know that our team is already reviewing this bug. Fixing these kinds of visual discomforts is important to us and we hope to resolve it as soon as possible.

Thanks again for your valuable feedback and for being part of the VibePunch community. We'll continue to do our best to improve all aspects of the game, including visuals and music selection, for an even better experience.

Keep Punching!

reviews.review_description:
Game is fun and has a solid base to build from but is still a little short of VR main stays like Beat Saber. Having completely silent rockets being shot at you doesn’t make much sense and increases the difficulty level without reason. It’s hard to find the rockets in 3-D space when you turn your head. 

The height of the rockets should be adjustable as well since they seem to always go a little bit higher than is comfortable for punching. Situations where dual rockets need to be punched could be improved by showing them in a different color. ￼

Rapid combos of rockets targeting the same location can also be very difficult to see because all the smoke trails have the exact same color. If you miss the launching point of the rockets, you will never know how many are being shot at you and won’t know when you can safely address additional targets. 

But to reiterate, the completely silent rocket shot at you is the biggest issue for me and is what’s holding back the game from becoming something that stays in my library for a long time.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the detailed feedback— we really appreciate the time you took to outline these points. We’ll review each item and explore ways to improve rocket audio cues, visibility, height adjustments, and trail differentiation. Your insights are extremely helpful as we continue refining the game, and we’ll work on solutions to make Vibe Punch a more intuitive and enjoyable experience. Thanks again for helping us make the game better!

reviews.review_description:
UPDATE: I’m changing my review from 4 stars to 5 stars ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️ ⭐️ After playing some of the other song packs, I see that overall this game is 👍👍. 



As a music/percussion guy, I’d like to offer some observations. I’m a little confused about why sometimes the hits sound off-beat (early or late). Is there a specific hit zone that you need to connect to, with the targets? It just feels very “off” when the “Hit” sound isn’t quantized with the beat of the music. Also, I find the bombs a little confusing. I’m sure they are designed to make you dodge to the left or right, but the approach trajectory leaves me guessing sometimes. Something that encourages you to squat occasionally, would also add to the fitness element. 

Speaking of sounds, the kick and snare sounds work well, feel satisfying. The hand-clap sound does not work very well, and feels empty, like punching air. It would be nice if there was a stronger sound to announce the 1st beat when you enter a new verse/bridge/chorus, etc. (POWWWWWW!) Ideally, there would be a choice for the player to select which Kick/Snare/Accent sounds they want. (Personally, I prefer a snare with more reverb.) 

Otherwise, great job on this concept. A lot of fun, which keeps me coming back, every day 👍👍

developer_response.body:
Thank you for enjoying VibePunch.
We appreciate your valuable feedback.
We believe it will greatly help our team improve the game and move forward to the next stage.

We are aware that the hit sound can sometimes be off-beat.
There are some technical difficulties, but we are trying to improve it.

We agree with your opinion that having more variety and richness in sound would be good.

VibePunch is currently undergoing various improvements and polishing in preparation for official release.
We expect you will see many improvements when the game launches.

Keep Punching!
